assumptions: The dividend receivable is an intragroup monetary item denominated in a currency different from the relevant functional currency.
The question concerns hedge accounting in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context identifies fair value hedge as a hedge of a recognised asset or liability and separately addresses foreign currency risk on intragroup monetary items in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context identifies cash flow hedge as a distinct hedge accounting model and also includes foreign currency hedging of intragroup transactions in consolidated financial statements.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: The context includes the separate hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk arising from a net investment in a foreign operation within consolidated financial statements.
